# Help Scout Contact-Based Pricing Pivot

In 2025, Help Scout moved from a seat-based pricing model ($/agent/month) to a contact-based model ($/contacts in your database/month). This was a significant architectural shift in their business model and had immediate implications for growing teams using Help Scout.

## Key findings

**Old model:** ~$20/agent/month (Plus plan). A 5-person support team paid $100/month regardless of conversation volume.

**New model (2025):** Pricing scales with the number of contacts stored in Help Scout (customers who have sent at least one email). Plans:
- Free: up to 100 contacts
- Standard: $50/month for up to 1,000 contacts
- Plus: $75/month for up to 2,500 contacts
- Pro: Custom for > 2,500 contacts

**Impact on growing teams:** A SaaS product with 5,000 active customers (even if only 500 contact support monthly) is now priced at the Pro tier. The contact count includes ALL historical contacts, not just active support users. This caught many existing Help Scout customers off-guard during migration.

**Community reaction:** Hacker News and Reddit /r/saas threads (November 2025) show significant churn among Help Scout's SMB customer base. Common migration destinations cited: Plain (developer teams), Intercom (growth-stage with AI needs), and Freshdesk (pure cost optimization).

**Help Scout's AI features:** Help Scout launched "AI Summarize" and "AI Assist" (response suggestions) in 2025. These are basic compared to Fin 2.0 — they are copilot features for agents, not autonomous deflection bots. Help Scout does not have an equivalent to Fin for AI-first ticket resolution.

**Shared inbox quality:** Help Scout remains the highest-quality shared inbox experience for email-first support teams. Features: collision detection (prevents two agents replying simultaneously), saved replies, tagging, and conversation views. Its Slack integration is notification-only (no bi-directional sync).

## Key takeaways

- Help Scout's contact-based pricing is now punishing for any team with > 2,500 historical contacts; plan selection must account for total contact database size, not current agent headcount.
- Help Scout is appropriate for early-stage products (< 500 contacts) or email-first teams that don't need AI deflection or Slack Connect.
- The 2025 pivot caused significant churn; review the migration guides before recommending Help Scout to a team that will scale quickly.
- Help Scout's AI features are copilot-level only; it is NOT a substitute for Fin or Ari for autonomous deflection.
- Solid Docs / Knowledge Base product included at no extra charge — one genuine advantage vs. Plain's paid add-on approach.
